title: Arthur the King Opening Weekend Update - $7.5 Million Disappointing Debut
text: Mark Wahlberg's Arthur the King had a disappointing box office debut, opening in third place with just $7.5 million during its first weekend. The film, based on a true story about an endurance racer and a stray dog, finished behind Kung Fu Panda 4 and Dune: Part Two. Despite the weak opening, the movie received encouraging audience reception with an A CinemaScore grade, suggesting strong word-of-mouth potential. Critics were harsher, giving it a 63% Rotten Tomatoes score, while audiences awarded it 98%. With a modest $19 million budget, the film isn't necessarily doomed. The article notes that upcoming competition from Ghostbusters: Frozen Empire and Luca could pose challenges, but positive audience sentiment could help it recover. Wahlberg's effort to meet the film's demanding training requirements ultimately didn't translate to immediate box office success, though the strong audience approval suggests the movie could still find financial success through its theatrical run.
